sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari have struggled recently, with no wins in their last 5 matches (1 draw, 1 loss in competitive games), scoring only 1 goal. Parma are also in poor form, with 1 draw and 1 loss in their last 2 competitive matches, conceding 3 goals while scoring just 1. Both teams show defensive vulnerabilities and limited attacking output.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Cagliari have dominated with 2 wins (2-1 and 3-2), scoring 5 goals to Parma's 3. Matches between these sides often feature goals from both teams, with both teams scoring in 6 of their last 7 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cagliari and Parma both rank low in key attacking metrics, with only 1 goal scored each this season. Defensively, Cagliari concede fewer big chances (4) compared to Parma (data suggests similar struggles). Cagliari’s home advantage and slightly better defensive stats give them a marginal edge.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Given the referee’s average of 4.9 yellow cards per game and historical head-to-head data showing over 4.5 cards in 5 of 7 matches, expect a physical game. Both teams average low corners (Cagliari 1.5, Parma similar), and recent trends suggest under 10.5 corners. Goals are likely late, based on seasonal distribution.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Unipol Domus saw Cagliari take a narrow lead over Parma with a scoreline of 1-0, thanks to a well-taken header by Yerry Mina in the 33rd minute. Cagliari dominated possession and showed intent in the final third, creating a couple of big chances, though they missed one significant opportunity. Parma, despite having less of the ball, were dangerous on the attack, managing several entries into the penalty area and squandering a pair of big chances themselves. Both teams displayed competitive physicality, with aerial and ground duels almost evenly split.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Cagliari's performance
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari showcased a solid first half, maintaining control of the ball for the majority of the time. Their defensive structure was robust, with Yerry Mina not only scoring but also contributing significantly at the back with key clearances. Players like Adam Obert stood out with creative contributions, setting up dangerous moments for the home side. However, their finishing could have been sharper, as they failed to convert one of their big opportunities. The team will look to capitalize on their possession advantage and home support in the second half to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Parma's response
halftimeSections.text: Parma, despite trailing, showed they can pose a real threat in attack. Their ability to penetrate the penalty area frequently highlights their attacking intent, even with lower possession. Patrick Cutrone was particularly active upfront but missed crucial chances that could have leveled the score. Midfielders like Adrián Bernabé played a pivotal role in creating opportunities with key passes. Defensively, they held their ground in duels but will need to tighten up to prevent Cagliari from further exploiting their chances in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Initially, Cagliari started with some early pressure, but Parma quickly took over with sustained attacking spells for a significant stretch. Midway through the half, Cagliari regained momentum with consistent pushes into the opposition half, culminating in the goal that shifted the tide in their favor. Towards the end of the half, Parma mounted a late surge, but it wasn’t enough to break through Cagliari’s defense. The back-and-forth nature of the attacking waves suggests an open second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas for second half
halftimeSections.text: For Cagliari, maintaining their possession and converting chances will be crucial to securing the win at home. They’ll need to watch for Parma’s counter-attacks, as the away side has shown capability in breaking into dangerous areas. Parma, on the other hand, must improve their finishing after missing key opportunities in the first half. Winning back possession in the final third could be a game-changer for them. Discipline will also be key, as both teams picked up a yellow card each and cannot afford to lose players to further bookings.
